items.title: Ex-Arizona guard Anthony Dell’Orso signs deal with Australian pro club
items.summary: TUCSON, ARIZONA - MARCH 02: Anthony Dell'orso #3 of the Arizona Wildcats reacts during the second half of the NCAAB game against the Iowa State Cyclones at McKale Center at ALKEME Arena on March 02, 2026 in Tucson, Arizona. The Wildcats defeated the Hawkeyes 73-57. (Photo by Christian Petersen/Getty Images) | Getty Images Anthony Dell’Orso came to the United States from Australia four years ago in hopes of improving his pro prospects. After a successful college career, one that culminated with helping Arizona return to the Final Four, he’s going back home.The ex-UA guard has signed a 3-year contract to play for the Perth Wildcats of the National Basketball League, the top professional league in Australia and New Zealand.The Perth Wildcats have agreed to terms with Australian guard Anthony Dell'Orso on a three-year contract, pending NBL approval! 🤝Fresh off a stint with the…

items.title: YouTube Gold: Kareem’s Choice For GOAT May Surprise You
items.summary: NEW YORK - UNDATED: Milwaukee Bucks' Oscar Robertson #1 dribbles against New York Knicks' Dick Barnett #12 during a game at Madison Square Garden in New York, New York. (Photo by Focus on Sport via Getty Images) | Focus on Sport via Getty Images Generally speaking, most people consider either Michael Jordan or LeBron James to be the GOAT of professional basketball. Jordan was an incredible presence from 1984 to 2003, minus his first two retirements. As for James, he started playing in the NBA in 2003 and, for better or worse, is still playing (he is not the player he used to be, and at some point, his diminishing skills will hurt his legacy). As we said, most people would go with one of them for the greatest of all time, but not everyone. Take Kareem Abdul-Jabbar. Kareem has a somewhat forgotten choice: Oscar Robertson. Robertson was somewhat overshadowed in the 1960s by the…

items.title: Lakers legend’s brutal reality check for LeBron as he keeps NBA waiting
items.summary: Shaquille O’Neal is weighing in on LeBron James’ NBA destiny.The retired athlete — who played eight seasons with the Los Angeles Lakers from 1996 to 2004 — addressed James’ decision to leave the California-based team ahead of his unprecedented 24th NBA season.“Go where you can win, LeBron. When it’s over for us, it’s over,” O’Neal, 54, exclusively told the California Post in a new interview. Shaquille O’Neal is weighing in on LeBron James’ NBA destiny. WireImage“Go where you can win, LeBron. When it’s over for us, it’s over,” O’Neal, 54, exclusively told the California Post in a new interview.  Andrew Schwartz / SplashNews.com“You never want to sit back and say, ‘I shoulda, I coulda, I woulda.’ I would say [go to] Cleveland, Golden State, Miami or even Philly.”James, 41, shocked the sports world in June when he announced he was leaving the Lakers…

items.title: LeBron James delaying free agency decision for one key reason
items.summary: The LeBron James free agency saga has yet to reach its denouement, and the latest report reveals the reason for the delay.James is reportedly “slow-playing” his decision to see if a suitor can trade for Anthony Davis or Kyrie Irving, according to Yahoo Sports writer Kevin O’Connor.LeBron James and Anthony Davis won an NBA title together in 2020. Getty ImagesDavis and Irving are both former teammates of the 41-year-old, who won two of his four NBA championships with said All-Stars. Sources believe that LeBron James is slow-playing his decision to see if a suitor can trade for Anthony Davis or Kyrie Irving, per @KevinOConnor. Should the Cavs trade for either if it means getting LeBron? pic.twitter.com/7UIkOv3PD4— ESPN Cleveland (@ESPNCleveland) July 22, 2026Now, it appears James is looking to reunite with one of the two as he pursues complete basketball happiness. A reunion with either…

items.title: Longtime Trail Blazers broadcaster Kevin Calabro parts ways with team due to 'subprime' offer
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers made headlines in May after the organization laid off about 70 people as part of a restructuring under new team owner Tom Dundon. The negative press kept coming Wednesday, when The Oregonian's Joe Freeman reported that longtime broadcaster Kevin Calabro was parting ways with the organization.Calabro wasn't the only one. He left under his own terms, but Forbes' Maury Brown also reported that "TV analyst Michael Holton and statistician Tom Haberstroh, as well as TV hosts Neil Everett and Jamie Hudson have all been let go."(Editor's note: Tom Haberstroh is a contributing writer at Yahoo Sports)Portland released a statement about the cuts to its broadcast teams. "We are deeply grateful to all the hard-working individuals who have contributed to our award-winning broadcasts over the years. I can't comment on individual personnel…

items.title: Caitlin Clark featured on NBA 2K27 video game cover with ‘weird’ twist
items.summary: Indiana Fever guard Caitlin Clark (22) dribbles the ball while New York Liberty guard Pauline Astier (18) defends in the first half at Gainbridge Fieldhouse.Fever star Caitlin Clark is going to be featured on the cover of NBA 2K27’s deluxe edition, but fans noticed a weird element to the otherwise cool and historic moment.Clark’s appearance on the cover marks the first time a WNBA player has been featured on her own global cover of the NBA edition of the game. However, several fans thought the cover didn’t exactly resemble Clark, with many accusing the creators of the game of using artificial intelligence for the digital drawing.Sports business journalist Darren Rovell commented, “Weird to put someone else’s face on her. They don’t do that for NBA cover athletes.”“Idk who that is but it’s certainly not Caitlin Clark,” another user posted on X.Indiana Fever guard Caitlin Clark (22)…

items.title: Did Adam Silver's remarks delay LeBron James' free agency choice?
items.summary: There's a logical explanation to why it's taking so long for LeBron James to announce his free agency decision on where he will play in the 2026-27 season.A Cleveland sports radio host believes that James has intentionally pushed back his announcement because he was "irritated" with comments made last week by NBA Commissioner Adam Silver.Cleveland sports radio personality Andy Baskin said Silver's comments "irritated" James and the former Los Angeles Lakers star decided to push back the announcement. Baskin, who has a radio show called Baskin and Phelps on 92.3 The Fan, went as far as to say that James was ready to announce his decision last week."LeBron's crew was probably ready to make an announcement, especially because they were in New York," Baskin said. "The world had their eyes on New York because of the World Cup as well. Yeah,…

items.title: Grizzlies, D'Angelo Russell expected to work together to allow veteran guard to find new home
items.summary: Grizzlies point guard D'Angelo Russell is not believed to be a long-term piece in Memphis, with both sides expected to work together toward a resolution that would allow the 30-year-old to find a new home at some point this offseason — whether via a buyout agreement or a trade — sources tell Yahoo Sports.Russell, who appeared in just 25 games last season in Dallas, arrived in Memphis earlier this month as part of an expansive six-team deal involving Milwaukee, Washington, Dallas, the LA Clippers and Detroit. The trade also sent center Isaiah Stewart, guard AJ Johnson, three future second-round picks, a conditional first-round pick and a second-round swap to the Grizzlies.The veteran guard was assumed to be part of a ball-handling group in Dallas last season designed to assist in the development of then-rookie Cooper Flagg, but started just three games — seemingly falling out of…
